title: Account access case after staged release exhausts session capacity
incident_summary: Session refresh and sign-in reliability are collapsing after a staged release, with capacity saturation spreading across regions.

alerts.title: DB pool saturation
alerts.content: Auth DB connection pool reached 100% and queue depth rising.

alerts.title: Auth CPU high
alerts.content: Auth CPU elevated due to request retries.

logs.title: Connection leak trace
logs.content: session-refresh path missing connection close in canary build.

logs.title: Replica lag warning
logs.content: Replica lag rose slightly but primary write path unaffected.

runbook_snippets.title: Canary rollback procedure
runbook_snippets.content: Disable canary traffic, recycle pods, and verify pool usage recovers.

runbook_snippets.title: Read replica scaling
runbook_snippets.content: Use for read saturation incidents, not connection leaks.

timeline_notes.title: Auth canary start
timeline_notes.content: Auth canary started 17 minutes before pool saturation alert.

red_herrings: Replica lag warnings suggest data-plane stress but do not explain connection leak signature.
true_root_cause: Connection leak in auth canary exhausted database pool
true_mitigation: Disable canary traffic and restart leaking auth pods
true_mitigation_aliases: rollback auth canary and recycle deployment
stop canary route then restart auth service
